life_history: Skyler has a history marked by feelings of emotional invalidation and a lack of being heard, stemming from early childhood experiences, such as being chastised for expressing emotions. These experiences have contributed to ongoing struggles with depression and a pervasive sense of unworthiness. Recent interactions with friends, particularly Edson, have triggered these historical feelings, leading to a cycle of withdrawal and avoidance in social situations.
core_belief_description: Unlovable: I am unlovable. I am undesirable, unwanted. I am bound to be rejected. I am bound to be abandoned. I am bound to be alone.
intermediate_beliefs: People will not find me worth their time.
If someone does not reach out to me, it means they do not value me.
If I am not explicitly included, I am unwanted.
My worth is contingent on others' acceptance.
intermediate_beliefs_during_depression: When I am depressed, no one will want to be around me. If I'm not perfect, people will reject me. My value to friends is conditional on my ability to be socially available and upbeat.
coping_strategies: Avoidance of social interactions, such as declining invitations to prevent potential rejection.
Seeking affirmation from others to combat feelings of unworthiness while simultaneously distancing from situations that could lead to emotional pain.

cognitive_models.situation: Seeing Edson's attempt to reinitiate contact after a period of silence.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: He only reaches out when it suits him; I am not valued.

cognitive_models.situation: Not being invited by a friend for a dinner with other friends.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: My friends don't want me around; I am forgotten and excluded.
cognitive_models.behavior: Expresses hurt feelings to friend, internalizes the situation as a rejection.
